title: WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN’S SHELTER - 211info
text: THE SALVATION ARMY WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN’S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to those escaping domestic violence. Crisis intervention services include emergency shelter, crisis line, advocacy, therapeutic children's programs, and support groups. Self-sufficiency programming includes transitional housing and advocacy, information and referral, and life skills training. Provides community education and educational materials. The program is 60 days with the potential of an extension to 90 days. Eligibility: Single women and women with children (male children must be under age 18) fleeing domestic violence, sexual assault, trafficking, intimate partner violence, or elder abuse Hours: Shelter hours and crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Client screenings: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 731-3900 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 731-3900 Main phone (503) 731-3910 Administration Hotline (503) 731-3900 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Call for information -Access to shelter: A safety and stabilization assessment must be completed prior to entry. Safety assessments to access shelter can be completed through the agency or with another agency (Call to Safety, Gateway Center, Raphael House, Bradley Angle, IRCO, NAYA, Ecumenical Ministries of Oregon, Volunteers of America, UNICA, YWCA). If the shelter is at capacity, applicants are encouraged to call back. The shelter does not maintain a waitlist. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties Services: Counseling for Children Affected by Domestic Violence Domestic Violence Hotlines Domestic Violence Hotlines * Mothers Domestic Violence Shelters * Women Domestic Violence Shelters * Women Domestic Violence Support Groups * Mothers Domestic Violence Support Groups * Women Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Prevention * Mothers Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Prevention * Women If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
